experiment: Iter-31 Wave A launched: calibration-selected temporal grain (auto-k). Six 100k-step probes (k=2/4/8 on Pick+Ori) test whether the SHORT-budget disc_err_gap predicts the known full-budget k-ordering; GO -> Wave B: auto-k TD-MPC2 vs vanilla on unseen tasks (the positive-method gate aimed at an ICLR-shaped result). CheetahRun OOS scored: +11% not separated = 'weak-positive' exactly as committed; predictor closes at 8/8 ordering facts at fixed k.
dev: Part 7 published; paper v2 (11pp, compiles) pushed to the Overleaf repo with the scored predictor + GO-asymmetry + n=8 anchor. Dev box next: harvest Wave A ckpts (~1h runs) -> short-budget signals -> GO/NO-GO for Wave B. Cartesian OOS dropped (its run saved no ckpts); cheetah carried the OOS test instead.
eta: Wave A probes ~1-1.5h each (6 runs, 5 workers) -> auto-k GO/NO-GO ~18:30 UTC; Wave B (if GO) queues tonight, finals tomorrow.

history.title: Part 1: From scratch to Phase 2 on HopperHop (world-model tutorial)
Part 2: The mechanism-check that saved a campaign (8 mirages)
Part 3: The latent was already the abstraction (value-sufficiency probe)
Part 4: The redundancy criterion — the paper (3/3 latent classes measured)
Part 5: Using abstraction right — aim at planning axes (iter-30 plan)
Part 6: Pre-registered predictions, published before results
Part 6 UPDATE: predictor scored — k2 4/4, k8 0/3, k=4 optimal
Part 7: The predictor transfers domains (weakly, as predicted)
